MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacie Nelson for Dominican Sisters Mission San Jose. Greg Spaulding Former Counsel for Co-Administrator. Martha Keller for and with Joseph Angelesco and for David Angelesco (Co-Administrator).

Counsel addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes and by the Court regarding ordinary fees/extraordinary fees. Ms. Keller and Mr. Spaulding explained the level/extent of services they provided. Ms. Nelson expressed her concerns. The matters were taken under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Continued To Mar-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacie Nelson for Domican Sisters of Mission San Jose (Beneficiaries). Martha Keller for and with Joseph Angelesco and for David Angelesco (Co-Administrators). Gregory Spaulding Former Counsel for the Co-Administrators.

There was no objection to disbursing the funds and said funds being used for the purpose of the retirement homes/care for the Sisters. The Court stated that the funds should be segregated and placed into a separate account. Counsel addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes, and presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to fees and the reserve amount. The accounting petition, and the petition for ordinary and extraordinary fees were taken under submission. Status of the Co-Administrators' compensation, the reserve and the former accountant's fees was set for March 22, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (Objections are to be filed by March 10, 2011 and responses are to be filed by March 17, 2011).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-25-2010 Continued To Nov-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacie Nelson for Dominican Sisters of Mission San Jose. Martha Keller for David Angelesco and for and with Joseph Angelesco (Co-Administrator). Kevin Ford for and with Joseph Angelesco (Creditor).

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation is to be completed by November 5, 2010. The matter was continued to November 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court reserved a trial date of January 21, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Continued To Aug-25-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marta Keller for Attorney Tommy Angel for David Angelesco and Joseph Angelesco (Co-Administrator). Betty Epstein for Dominican Sister of San Jose (Beneficiaries). Kevin Ford for Joseph Angelesco.

Ms. Keller stated that Ms. Angel substituted in for Mr. McCullough. Counsel agreed to continue the matter in order to have the opportunity to conduct discovery. The matter was continued to August 25, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
